info.name: Super Banking — PingOne Test Endpoints
info.description:
All `/api/pingone-test/*` BFF endpoints for the Super Banking demo.

## How to authenticate (session-based endpoints)

The BFF uses OAuth Authorization Code + PKCE which requires a real browser. Postman cannot complete this flow directly.

1. **Log in via browser** — open `https://api.ping.demo:4000` and sign in (e.g. bankuser / Tigers7&).
2. **Copy the session cookie** — in DevTools → Application → Cookies → `api.ping.demo`, copy the `connect.sid` value.
3. **Paste into Postman** — set the `sessionCookie` collection variable to the copied value (including the `s%3A` prefix).
4. **Send 🔑 Token Operations requests** — the Cookie header is pre-configured.

No-session endpoints (🔧 Setup, 📋 Discovery, 🛠️ Update/Fix) work without authentication.

variable.description: Paste connect.sid cookie value from browser DevTools

item.name: 🔐 Authentication (read me)
item.description:
OAuth login requires a real browser — Postman cannot complete the PKCE redirect flow.

**Steps:**
1. Open {{frontendUrl}} in a browser and log in.
2. DevTools → Application → Cookies → copy `connect.sid` value.
3. Set the `sessionCookie` collection variable to that value.
4. 🔑 Token Operations requests will use it automatically.

item.name: ⚠️ READ ME — How to get a session cookie

request.description:
This request just opens the frontend. It is here as a reminder:

1. Open {{frontendUrl}} in your **browser** (not Postman).
2. Sign in as `bankuser` / `Tigers7&` (user) or your admin account.
3. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies → `api.ping.demo`.
4. Copy the full `connect.sid` value (including `s%3A` prefix).
5. In Postman: Collection Variables → set `sessionCookie` to the copied value.

All 🔑 Token Operations requests include a `Cookie: connect.sid={{sessionCookie}}` header.

item.name: 🔧 Setup & Config
item.description: Configuration and worker-token endpoints. No login required — these use the worker (client_credentials) app.

request.description: Returns the current effective configuration (env + configStore). Shows all PingOne client IDs, audiences, and feature flags.
Obtains a worker client_credentials token from PingOne. Verifies worker app connectivity.
Updates worker token credentials in configStore + .env. Only needed if the default worker credentials are wrong.
Verifies PingOne assets: environment reachable, apps exist, resource servers configured.

item.name: 📋 Discovery (Read-Only)
item.description: Read-only endpoints. No login required — these use the worker token internally.

request.description: Lists all PingOne applications in the environment. Shows app types, client IDs, enabled status.
Fetches only AI_AGENT type apps. Flags Super Banking ones. Reports missing expected apps.
Lists all PingOne resource servers — names, audiences, types.
Lists scopes on all resource servers, grouped by RS.
Lists PingOne users. Auto-sets {{userId}} from the first user returned.
Diagnoses token exchange: checks MCP RS, scopes, exchanger app grants. Returns canExchange true/false and lists missingFromRS / missingFromApp.

item.name: 🛠️ Update / Fix (Mutating)
item.description: Idempotent — safe to run repeatedly. These CREATE or MODIFY PingOne resources via the worker token.

request.description: Ensures Banking RS and MCP RS exist with all canonical scopes. Creates RS if missing; adds only missing scopes.
Adds missing canonical scopes to existing RS. Skips RS creation.
Grants correct banking scope subsets to each Super Banking app. Returns per-app steps + AI_AGENT apps discovered.

Two-step: (1) PATCHes mayAct.sub on user, (2) ensures may_act attribute mapping on OIDC apps with correct SpEL. Auto-fixes broken ${user.mayAct} mappings.

Run GET /users first to populate {{userId}}.
Clears mayAct on the user. No mapping step.
Auto-fixes MCP token exchange: creates MCP RS if missing, adds scopes, assigns to exchanger app. Run diagnose-mcp-exchange first.
Creates or repairs the Banking API resource server and its canonical scopes.

item.name: 🔑 Token Operations (session-based)
item.description:
These endpoints read tokens from the BFF session. Set the `sessionCookie` collection variable first (see 🔐 Authentication folder).

**Prerequisite:** Log in via browser and copy `connect.sid` into the `sessionCookie` variable.

request.description: Shows decoded claims of the session's access token (token value is truncated for security). Confirms login type: admin / customer / ai_agent.
Gets the agent's client_credentials token (MCP Token Exchanger or AI Agent app). No user session needed.
Exchanges the session user's access token → MCP token (single RFC 8693 exchange). Returns full decoded MCP token with claims.
Exchanges the session user's ID token (not AT) → MCP token.
Exchange with actor: user AT + agent CC → MCP token. Shows act/may_act claims.
Full 2-exchange delegation chain: (1) user AT → agent AT, (2) agent AT + actor → final MCP token. Shows full delegation claim chain.
